avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Weak windslab will gain depth and become extensive on most lee slopes. Poor stability on all steep North-West through North to East aspects above 800m. Newly formed weak cornices will hang over many of these locations. Much better stability on all windward aspects and ridges. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Heavy overnight snowfall turning showery by dawn. Strong South-South-East winds easing to moderate in the morning and becoming South-South-Westerly later. Very cold overnight, freezing level rising to 750m by midday.
Observed Weather Influences: Dry, cold and clear with plenty of sunshine in the early part of the day. Light South-West winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Good stability, hard and icy in many locations. Localised accumulations of windslab were present on a few steep Southerly aspects above 950m; sustained sunshine brought some early consolidation to surface layers in these places. Areas of shallow moderately bonded older cold windslab lie on some steep heavily shaded West to North- West facing aspects above the same altitude. The avalanche hazard is Low

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Snowfall and drifting will form moderately to weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations on mainly West through North to North-East aspects, with some cross-loading occurring. Gully tops and corrie rims will be worst affected. Shallower areas of windslab will form in wind sheltered locations below this altitude. New fragile cornices will start to form. A good deal of the snow will be firm and wind scoured. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall above 300 metres, arriving by late evening. Very light snowfall during the day, eventually only above 650 metres. Winds will be gale force Southerly, then South-South-Easterly during the heaviest of the snowfall, easing to fresh Southerly during the day. 400 metre freezing level overnight, rising to 700 metres by mid-afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Dry, bright and sunny morning. High level cloud building in the afternoon. Light variable winds during the morning, fresh South-South-Westerly by early afternoon. Freezing level down into the glens.
ObservedSnowStability: A good deal of the snow is firm and well bonded. Localised areas of moderately bonded snow exist in wind sheltered locations only, on South-East through South-West to North-West aspects above 800 metres. Gully tops and corrie rims most affected. Exposed locations are wind scoured and very icy.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New snow and wind transport of existing snow and rime will form weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations, greatest accumulations are expected to form above 850 metres in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a mainly North-West through North to North-Easterly aspect, cross loading may affect other aspects, fragile cornices may form above similar areas.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: New snow overnight and through Sunday with potential to be heavy will affect slopes mainly above 600 metres, winds will be generally South-South-Easterly gale force overnight becoming moderate to fresh South-South-Westerly through Sunday. The freezing level overnight will be around 400 metres rising to 800 by morning.
Observed Weather Influences: Cold, dry and settled conditions have affected the area with an overnight frost at all levels, winds have been light to moderate South to South-Westerly.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is generally frozen hard and stable in most areas. Localised areas of unstable windslab exist on steeper slopes with a mainly Westerly and North-Westerly aspect above 950 metres.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh unstable windslab will develop through the period mainly on North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres. Sheltered locations on West and East aspects will also be affected. Fragile cornices will be found above these aspects. On other aspects the snowpack will be mostly firm, scoured and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force South-South-Easterly winds overnight will ease and veer to become fresh from the South-South-West. Overnight snow above 300 metres followed by showers falling as snow above around 700 metres. The freezing level will rise to around 800 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a dry day with light but increasing Southerly winds. The freezing level rose to around 400 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The older snowpack remains firm and stable. There is a surface layer of soft unconsolidated snow to low levels which has good stability. Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab are present around high summits and ridges. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Poorly bonded windslab will develop especially during Saturday night with avalanches likely on steep wind sheltered North-West to North-East aspects above 900 metres.  Cross loading will affect West and East facing slopes. On these aspects fragile cornices will build. Below 900 metres, localised unstable windslab will develop on the same aspects on steep banks and burn lines. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force South then South-South-Easterly winds overnight with snowfall above 200 metres. During the day, South-South-Westerly winds will gradually ease strong to fresh with light snow showers above 600 metres. The 500 metre freezing level will rise to 800 metres at dawn where it will remain for the rest of the forecast period.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry sunny day. Light to moderate Westerly winds backed towards the South through the afternoon. Summit temperatures were around minus 8 Celsius for the duration of the day.
ObservedSnowStability: New accumulations of windslab have developed on East to South-East aspects mainly above 1100 metres. Moderately bonded snow was evident on Westerly to Northerly aspects above 950 metres.  Areas most affected are in steep locations such as coire rims, gully tops, crag aprons and convexities. A widespread friable crust persists on many aspects above 1000 metres but in most areas this is bridged by dense consolidating windslab. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Overnight and through the day areas of unstable windslab will be deposited on sheltered locations on mainly North-West to North East aspects above about 800 metres. Sheltered locations on other aspects may be affected by cross loading. Fresh cornices will be unstable. However, exposed windward slopes will remain scoured and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Storm force Southerly winds will become lighter during the day. Heavy snow overnight will ease through the day. The freezing level will rise to about 800 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day, sunny during the morning then cloudy in the afternoon. After a calm and frosty morning the Southerly wind strengthened and the freezing level rose to around 500 metres during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Little change is happening within the snowpack. In many locations it is firm, icy and stable. However, areas of moderately bonded windslab remain in some sheltered South-West through West to North aspects above 1000 metres. The distribution of this windslab is quite variable, with some of locations indicated holding little windslab. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
